hero.title: Land Degradation Neutrality (LDN)
hero.description: Learn how Africa is addressing land degradation through Land Degradation Neutrality (LDN). Explore challenges, indicators, and monitoring tools supporting SDG 15.3.1.
hero.tagline: Halt and reverse land degradation. Restore ecosystems. Build resilience.

whatIsLdn.paragraph1After: is a global commitment to ensure that land degradation is balanced by restoration, so that the overall amount of healthy and productive land remains stable or increases over time.
whatIsLdn.paragraph2Before: In Africa, where land is central to livelihoods, food systems, and ecosystems, achieving
whatIsLdn.paragraph2After: is essential for sustainable development and climate resilience.

whatIsLdnCard.heading: What is Land Degradation Neutrality (LDN)?
whatIsLdnCard.unccdLinkText: United Nations Convention to Combat Desertification (UNCCD)
whatIsLdnCard.paragraph1After: . It aims to maintain or improve the quantity and quality of land resources needed to support ecosystem functions and human well-being.

landDegradationInAfrica.heading: Land Degradation in Africa
landDegradationInAfrica.description: Land degradation is a major environmental and socio-economic challenge across Africa.
landDegradationInAfrica.driver1: Climate change and increasing drought frequency;
landDegradationInAfrica.driver3: Deforestation and loss of vegetation cover;

keyFacts.heading: Key Facts
keyFacts.fact1: Over 65% of productive land in Africa is affected by degradation.
keyFacts.fact2: More than 40% of the population depends directly on land resources.
keyFacts.fact3: Land degradation significantly impacts food security and rural livelihoods.
keyFacts.fact4: It contributes to biodiversity loss and climate vulnerability.

monitoring.heading: Monitoring LDN in Africa
monitoring.paragraph1: Monitoring Land Degradation Neutrality in Africa is based on a set of globally agreed indicators, complemented by a broader framework under the UNCCD Performance Review and Assessment of Implementation System (PRAIS). This allows countries to assess land condition and track progress over time.
monitoring.indicatorsLabel: At the global level, LDN monitoring relies on three key indicators:
monitoring.indicator1: Land cover change, which tracks transitions between forests, croplands, grasslands, and other land types.
monitoring.indicator2: Land productivity dynamics, which measure vegetation health and biological productivity.
monitoring.indicator3: Soil organic carbon, which indicates soil quality and carbon storage capacity.
monitoring.combinedIndicators: These indicators are combined to produce the SDG Indicator 15.3.1: "Proportion of degraded land over total land area".

praisFramework.heading: Beyond Core Indicators: The PRAIS Framework:
praisFramework.description: In addition to the three core indicators, countries report through the UNCCD PRAIS system, which includes a wider set of indicators and information to assess progress toward LDN.
praisFramework.item1: Policy and institutional indicators, integrating LDN into national strategies.
praisFramework.item2: Land restoration and sustainable land management actions.
praisFramework.item4: Implementation and reporting metrics supporting national and global assessments.

implementation.heading: How LDN is implemented in Africa
implementation.introLabel: African countries are actively working toward achieving LDN through:
implementation.action2: Integrating LDN into national policies and strategies;
implementation.action3: Implementing land restoration and sustainable land management practices;

platformSupport.heading: How this platform supports LDN
platformSupport.intro: The Africa's Land Degradation Monitoring Platform plays a key role in supporting LDN implementation by providing:
platformSupport.feature2Text: Tools to monitor land degradation trends over time
platformSupport.feature4Text: Dashboards for SDG 15.3.1 reporting and analysis
platformSupport.feature5Text: Decision-support systems for policymakers and stakeholders

whyLdnMatters.heading: Why LDN matters for Africa
whyLdnMatters.introLabel: Achieving Land Degradation Neutrality is essential to:
whyLdnMatters.reason5: Improve livelihoods for millions of people.
whyLdnMatters.closingText: LDN is not only an environmental goal, but it is also a development priority for Africa!

exploreTools.heading: Explore LDN monitoring data and tools

ldnInAfricaBox.heading: Land Degradation Neutrality in Africa
ldnInAfricaBox.subtitle: Global framework, regional action, and country-level implementation

conceptPromotion.heading: Concept promotion
conceptPromotion.unccdLinkText: United Nations Convention to Combat Desertification (UNCCD)
conceptPromotion.paragraph2Before: That same year, LDN was adopted as
conceptPromotion.sdgLinkText: Sustainable Development Goals (SDG 15 - Life on Land)
conceptPromotion.sdgQuote: Combat desertification, restore degraded land and soil, including land affected by desertification, drought and floods, and strive to achieve a land degradation-neutral world by 2030.

globalProgress.heading: Global progress on LDN Targets
globalProgress.intro: Countries worldwide are actively engaging in the LDN target-setting process:
globalProgress.item1: 130+ countries are committed to setting LDN targets;
globalProgress.item2: 82 countries reported on voluntary LDN targets (2022 reporting cycle);
globalProgress.item3: 64 countries have published high-level LDN target notes.
globalProgress.closing: Additional countries are currently developing or updating their targets

supportingImplementation.heading: Supporting LDN implementation
supportingImplementation.intro: International and regional partners play a key role in supporting countries, including:
supportingImplementation.rcmrdLinkText: RCMRD (Regional Centre for Mapping of Resources for Development)

countryProfiles.heading: Country profiles and decision support
countryProfiles.intro: Each participating country develops an LDN country profile, providing:
countryProfiles.item3: Strategic guidance to support investment and policy decisions.

africaEngagement.heading: Africa's engagement
africaEngagement.intro: African countries are strongly engaged in the LDN process, with many:
africaEngagement.action3LinkText: land restoration and sustainable land management initiatives
